behaviors.description: Reject webhook when `X-Signature-256` HMAC does not match raw body; do not forward event.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is handler+signature verifier+queue handoff (MOCK-MISS: cross-module decision and side-effect suppression). No real OS/network/subprocess is required; in-process HTTP handler test with wired modules is sufficient (REAL-INFRA no). No AI output dependency (LLM-DEP no). Rejected System because behavior text is only about 401 + no forward, not real network conditions. Rejected Agentic/Workflow because no model decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Run the webhook route in-process with real signature middleware and a real queue adapter interface implementation (spyable in-memory test double). Send a request with mismatched signature and raw body bytes. Assert HTTP 401 and assert queue `enqueue` was never called / no record persisted.

behaviors.description: Persist event to queue before returning HTTP 200 acknowledgment.
behaviors.justification: This is ordering across modules (HTTP handler -> persistent queue write -> response), so Unit is too low (MOCK-MISS). Needs module wiring plus persistence boundary, but not real external network/subprocess (REAL-INFRA no). LLM and multi-agent steps are irrelevant. Rejected System because behavior text is about write-before-ack ordering, which is testable with in-process server and local persistence adapter.
behaviors.test_description: Use in-process request test against the real handler wired to a test persistent queue backend (e.g., sqlite/file-backed test DB). Instrument queue write completion and response send timestamps/events. Assert queue write commit completes before HTTP 200 is emitted.

behaviors.description: Return HTTP response without waiting for downstream event processing to finish.
behaviors.justification: Failure appears at module interaction between handler, queueing, and async processor lifecycle (MOCK-MISS yes). Can be caught fully in-process with controlled slow processor promise; no real OS/network requirement (REAL-INFRA no). No model dependency or multi-agent chain. Rejected System because behavior text only requires non-blocking async handoff, not real HTTP/network semantics.
behaviors.test_description: Wire handler to queue plus a downstream processor that intentionally blocks (deferred promise). Send valid webhook and assert response returns before processor promise resolves. Then resolve processor and verify eventual processing occurred.

behaviors.description: Response must arrive within 3 seconds wall-clock even under concurrent slow downstream processing of earlier events.
behaviors.justification: Behavior is explicitly wall-clock latency under concurrency; real scheduling/backpressure effects can be hidden by mocks/fake timers (MOCK-HIDE yes). Requires real HTTP timing and runtime behavior, which crosses into System (REAL-INFRA yes). No AI dependency or agent pipeline. Rejected Integration because behavior text explicitly requires "within 3 seconds wall-clock" under concurrent load, which is a real runtime characteristic.
behaviors.test_description: Start the service process with real HTTP listener and real queue/worker settings. Preload slow-processing events to create contention, then concurrently send new valid webhook requests over HTTP and measure end-to-end response time. Assert p95 (or strict per-request bound per spec) stays under 3 seconds.

behaviors.description: Do not write an event to queue if its event ID has already been received.
behaviors.justification: Dedup correctness usually depends on interaction between handler idempotency logic and persistent queue uniqueness/state (MOCK-MISS yes). No need for real external network/subprocess; local persistence wiring is enough (REAL-INFRA no). No AI dependency/multi-step agentic flow. Rejected System because behavior text is about duplicate-ID state handoff/persistence, not OS/network behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Use real handler + persistent test queue store with dedupe key/constraint. Submit same event ID twice via in-process HTTP calls. Assert first is persisted, second is rejected/skipped, and queue contains exactly one record for that ID.

behaviors.description: If queue reports full, return HTTP 503 and include `Retry-After` header.
behaviors.justification: This behavior is mapping of queue backpressure signal to HTTP response contract across module boundary (queue -> handler). Unit may miss wiring issues (MOCK-MISS yes), but real OS/network is not required because the behavior text says "when the queue reports it is full" (can be triggered via in-process adapter). No AI dependency. Rejected System using explicit disqualifier text: the condition is a reported queue state, not real network/OS failure.
behaviors.test_description: Wire handler to queue implementation/test double that returns a `QueueFull` result. Send valid webhook request in-process. Assert HTTP 503 and `Retry-After` header is present with configured value; assert event not persisted.

behaviors.description: Persistent queue enforces configured capacity and rejects writes that exceed limit.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is queue module + persistence backend + capacity config interaction; this is beyond single-function logic (MOCK-MISS yes). Usually testable with local DB/filesystem-backed queue in-process, so Integration is sufficient. No real external API/subprocess required, and no AI dependence. Rejected System because behavior text is about configured queue capacity enforcement, not OS-level or network behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Instantiate real persistent queue backend with small capacity (e.g., 2). Enqueue up to limit and assert success; enqueue one additional event and assert explicit rejection/error with no extra persisted record.
